meta.title: Belle Isle Local Government — Topic Archive
meta.note: Topic-centric historical record. Each topic is a thread with a chronological timeline of meeting events and recorded commissioner remarks. The Monday refresh appends new timeline entries to existing topics (matching by id) and creates new topics as they arise; it also appends a weekly-summary entry to 'weeks'. Prior entries are never edited. Commissioner remarks are summarized/quoted from official meeting minutes; verify against the linked source before relying on exact wording.

topics.title: FY2026-27 Budget & Millage
topics.summary: The annual budget and property-tax rate. Started the spring with no millage set and a compensation study driving costs up; by July a draft proposed raising the rate from 4.7845 to 5.0000 mills, set for a maximum-rate vote July 21 and final hearings in September.

timeline.event: FY27 process kicks off — cost pressure, no millage yet
timeline.detail: First draft budgets expected in June. The approved compensation study is the dominant cost pressure; no millage rate discussed yet. Pending reimbursements noted: Sol Avenue stormwater (~$196K anticipated), Hurricane Ian FEMA (pending, no timeline), Hurricane Irma closeout (~$140K).

remarks.text: There will be no cost savings; implementation will increase costs — initial estimates exceeded $300,000, with the largest impact in the Police Department.
Requested a historical comparison of prior interim pay adjustments to evaluate gaps between past adjustments, current recommendations, and job descriptions.

timeline.detail: Annual financial report, planning for FY26-27 budget meetings, a revenue update (reimbursements and fees), and a property-tax reform update. No proposed millage or spending figures finalized yet.

timeline.event: Draft budget with proposed millage increase
timeline.detail: Draft proposes millage of 5.0000 mills (up from 4.7845) — an ~8% increase in ad valorem revenue to ~$5.76M; General Fund $10.88M (+2%); ending fund balance $2.55M (23.4%, near the 25% target). Police +6% ($69,400 equipment, $57K capital); Public Works -27% (vehicle leasing vs. ownership); solid waste +7% (landfill +26%); $250K to Jade Pump Station; 4% COLA; medical insurance +8%. Five-year capital plan totals $11.03M.

Council takes up the proposed maximum millage for FY26/27 — the ceiling printed on TRIM notices. The final rate can be lowered at the September hearings but not raised. Agenda also includes discussion of statutory limitations on property-tax ballot questions.
Two statutory public hearings in September set the final millage and adopt the budget. Watch this thread for the adopted rate and any changes from the 5.0000 ceiling.

topics.title: Subdivisions, Lot Splits & Flag Lots
topics.summary: Amends Ch. 50 & 54 to define flag-shaped lots, restrict lot splits involving non-conforming uses, and require deed restrictions when flag-shaped lots are created.

timeline.detail: Introduced for first reading and continued to the August 4 meeting. Not yet law (Belle Isle ordinances require two readings).
Scheduled to return August 4 for continued consideration / possible second reading.

topics.title: Fire Rescue Assessment
topics.summary: Would establish the authority and procedures for a special assessment to fund fire-rescue service.

timeline.detail: Introduced for first reading. Establishes special-assessment authority and procedures for fire-rescue funding. Not yet law.

topics.title: Updated Purchasing Policy
topics.summary: Updates the city purchasing policy and repeals prior resolutions 20-08 and 25-02.

timeline.detail: Resolution to adopt an updated purchasing policy, repealing Res. 20-08 and 25-02. Resolutions pass in a single vote.

topics.title: Water Supply Facilities Work Plan
topics.summary: State-required update to the city's 10-year water supply plan. Reviewed by the Planning & Zoning Board, adopted by Council on final reading June 16 (6-0); requires state review before final effect.

timeline.detail: Advanced to second reading and adopted. Motion by Comm. Shuck, seconded by Comm. Oller; unanimous 6-0.

remarks.text: This is the same water supply plan amendment previously reviewed by the Planning & Zoning Board; it requires state review before final adoption.

topics.title: Flock Safety Camera Expansion
topics.summary: 13 Condor pan-tilt-zoom cameras at $52,000/year, approved June 16 (6-0) with a 90-day evaluation period. Commissioners raised privacy questions about cameras in parks and continuous recording.

timeline.detail: Flock Safety agreement for 13 Condor PTZ cameras at $52,000 annually. Motion by Comm. Lowell, seconded by Comm. Lance; unanimous 6-0. Includes a 90-day evaluation period.

remarks.text: Raised concern about cameras in parks — 'one resident said they are not a fan of their kid being on camera at all times' — and requested a public meeting where residents could see demonstrations and ask questions.
Asked whether the cameras record continuously.
Yes, they are always recording. We do not have a dedicated staff reviewing the live footage.

topics.title: School Resource Officers — Cornerstone Charter
topics.summary: FY26/27 agreement placing two school resource officers at Cornerstone Charter Academy; the school reimburses roughly 75%. Approved June 16 (6-0).

timeline.detail: SRO agreement with Cornerstone Charter Academy for 2026-2027; mayor authorized to execute. Motion by Comm. Lowell, seconded by Comm. Lance; unanimous 6-0.

remarks.text: Salary and benefits rose from ~$70,000 to ~$76,000. Total annual cost for two SRO positions is ~$250,000, with the school reimbursing ~$187,000 (about 75%).

topics.title: IT Services Contract — EOLA Technology
topics.summary: RFP 2026-04 awarded to EOLA Technology, a three-year agreement with two possible one-year renewals. Council emphasized holding the vendor accountable to contract terms.

timeline.detail: RFP 2026-04 awarded to EOLA Technology; city manager and attorney authorized to negotiate a three-year agreement with two possible one-year renewals. Motion by Comm. Lance, seconded by Comm. Oller; unanimous 6-0.

remarks.text: Recommended requiring the vendor to resolve issues under contract rather than staff resolving problems internally.
Response time had been a concern in the past, but service and responsiveness have improved significantly over the last six to eight months.
The City must hold the provider accountable to the terms of the contract.

topics.title: Citywide Annexation Study
topics.summary: Staff is recommending an RFP for an Annexation Strategy, Feasibility & Fiscal Impact Study evaluating contiguous unincorporated areas. Early-stage, no vote yet.

timeline.detail: City Manager's cover memo recommends preparing an RFP for a Citywide Annexation Strategy, Feasibility & Fiscal Impact Study of contiguous unincorporated areas. Discussion item; no vote.

topics.title: Stormwater — Jade Pump Station & Drainage
topics.summary: Stormwater capital work, including $250K for Jade Pump Station rehabilitation in the FY27 draft budget and a pending ~$196K reimbursement on the Sol Avenue project.

timeline.detail: Committee noted an anticipated ~$196,000 reimbursement tied to the Sol Avenue stormwater project among pending revenues.

timeline.event: $250K for Jade Pump Station in draft budget
timeline.detail: The FY27 draft dedicates $250,000 from the Stormwater Fund to rehabilitate the Jade Pump Station, part of an $11.03M five-year capital plan that also includes swale and drainage work.

weeks.label: Week of July 13–19, 2026
weeks.intro: A budget-heavy stretch: the Budget Committee wrapped its review of a proposed millage increase, and next Tuesday's council meeting sets the maximum tax rate for the year and takes up a new fire-rescue assessment.

highlights.line: Draft budget proposes raising millage to 5.0000 (from 4.7845); Council sets the maximum rate July 21.
New Fire Rescue Assessment ordinance (26-05) gets its first reading July 21.
Lot-split / flag-lot ordinance (26-03) continued to Aug 4 after its July 7 first reading.
Updated purchasing policy (Res. 26-06) on the July 21 agenda.
